# BetaWidgets Inc

Primary comparable. Smaller (~$1.2B revenue vs our $2.1B), similar business mix, traded as BWGT on NASDAQ. The competitor we get benchmarked against on every earnings call.

The asymmetry that matters: we run a more diversified customer book and a more efficient distribution footprint. They run a punchier sales motion and (until recently) better margins on commodity widgets. That gap is closing. See [margin-trend](#margin-trend) below.

## Snapshot

- HQ: Charlotte, NC
- ~$1.2B FY25 revenue, was 16.8% adj. EBITDA margin (down from 19.1% prior year)
- Public, NASDAQ: BWGT, mid-cap ~$1.9B market cap
- Two segments: Distribution (~78% rev) and Precision Solutions (~22% rev)
- Customer concentration: top 1 = 23.4% (Globex Manufacturing — confirmed [2026-05-09](../daily-notes/2026-05-09.md))

See also: [ACME Corp](./acme-corp.md) for the head-to-head positioning, [sector report](../external/sector-report-q1-2026.html#betawidgets) for third-party take.

## Margin trend

This is where the thesis lives or dies. Gross margin compressed 380bps yoy in Q1 2026 — they called it "transitory mix shift and input cost normalization." I don't buy it.

The math doesn't add up:

- Reported gross margin: 31.2% in Q1, vs 35.0% prior year
- Raw materials line +14% yoy
- Stated mix shift: management says favorable (higher precision mix) — that should *help* margins
- Inventory days: 71, up from 58. building inventory implies they're not selling through

If the mix were genuinely favorable, gross margin would expand, not compress 380bps. The simplest explanation: they're absorbing input cost increases they can't pass through. That's a *pricing power* problem, not a mix problem.

See annotated reasoning in [2026-05-08 daily](../daily-notes/2026-05-08.md#morning).

## Customer concentration

Confirmed on [2026-05-09](../daily-notes/2026-05-09.md). Top customer is Globex Manufacturing — 23.4% of revenue.

| Customer | % of FY25 Revenue | Trend |
|---|---|---|
| Globex Manufacturing | 23.4% | growing (was 19.0% in FY22) |
| (top 5 total) | 51.8% | growing |
| Long tail | 48.2% | shrinking |

Concentration is *getting worse*, not better. This is a thesis-grade risk. Source: [sector report appendix B](../external/sector-report-q1-2026.html#appendix-b-customer-concentration).

By contrast [ACME's top customer is ~9.5%](./acme-corp.md#customer-concentration). The diversity gap is the most durable competitive advantage we have, and the street consistently underweights it.

## Precision Solutions

BW's smaller higher-growth segment, directly comparable to [our Precision Components](./acme-corp.md#precision-components-segment). They got there earlier (acquired in 2015), so they have more programs in steady-state, but they've slowed on M&A since 2022.

- ~$264M FY25 revenue
- ~21% segment EBITDA margin (vs our 23%)
- Aerospace exposure ~60% (similar to ours)
- Medical device exposure ~15% (we don't compete here yet)

The medical device exposure is interesting. See [Marcus Reyes](../people/marcus-reyes.md)'s M&A target list for adjacencies.

## Catalysts

- **Q2 2026 earnings** — guidance call [2026-05-19](../daily-notes/2026-05-08.md). Will reveal whether margin compression is "transitory" or structural.
- **Globex contract renewal** — rumored late 2026. Outcome could move the stock ±15%.
- **CEO transition** — current CEO retiring end of 2026, no announced successor. Distraction risk.
- **Tariff resolution** — bilateral, not company-specific, but BW more exposed than ACME due to single-sourced commodity inputs.
